codeReview.summary: Feature broadly implements required public and admin glossary capabilities, but there is a blocking invalid-ID handling bug in the admin edit page and a non-blocking stored-XSS risk in public glossary definition rendering.

issues.description: Invalid edit IDs are parsed with parseInt() and only checked with isNaN(). Inputs like '/admin/kb/glossary/edit/1abc' are treated as ID 1 instead of being rejected/redirected, violating the invalid-ID redirect requirement. Use strict numeric validation (e.g., /^\d+$/) before fetching.

Public glossary definitions are rendered with dangerouslySetInnerHTML from admin-controlled content, while create/update API paths for glossary terms do not sanitize HTML. This creates a stored-XSS risk if malicious HTML is saved in gDefinition.

sharedStateObservations.observation: fullstack-worker skill procedure says to write UI component tests first, but this feature added only server tests while still marking followedProcedure=true. The skill procedure and practical enforcement are out of sync.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md Step 6 requires UI component tests; handoff tests.added includes only app/apps/server/src/__tests__/glossary.test.ts; handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true with no deviations.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared conventions do not explicitly require strict client-side route-param validation for numeric IDs, which likely contributed to permissive parseInt-based handling.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md and .factory/library/architecture.md describe API and form conventions but do not document strict numeric route-param checks; AdminGlossaryFormPage.tsx:57 uses parseInt(id, 10) + isNaN only.

summary: Result: fail. The implementation is mostly complete for glossary public/admin behavior, but admin edit invalid-ID handling is not strict enough (blocking). Also noted a non-blocking stored-XSS risk in glossary definition rendering and two shared-state documentation/procedure gaps.
